blurb:
This is my go-to spot when I'm craving proper Indian food and don't want to mess around with watered-down versions. It's in a strip mall off Perimeter Center, nothing fancy, but they know what they're doing with traditional dishes served family-style. Perfect for feeding a crowd or getting takeout when you want real flavors.

The biryani is obviously what they're known for, and it's worth the hype. Get the chicken or goat if you're feeling adventurous. The portions are generous, so one order easily feeds two people. They also do a solid job with their curries and the naan comes out hot and fresh.

Just know they close between lunch and dinner most days, so don't show up at 3pm expecting to eat. Weekend hours are longer, which is nice for lazy Saturday dinners.
address: 130 Perimeter Center Pl, Dunwoody, GA 30346, USA
hours: Monday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM, Tuesday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM, Wednesday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM, Thursday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM, Friday: 11:30 AM – 11:30 PM, Saturday: 9:00 AM – 11:30 PM, Sunday: 9:00 AM – 9:30 PM

restaurant_name: Mister O1 Extraordinary Pizza Sandy Springs
blurb:
This place has ruined regular pizza for me. The crust is so thin and light it's almost like eating a crispy cloud, and they use ingredients that actually taste like something. It's become our default Friday night dinner because the kids love it and we don't feel guilty about the quality.

The Margherita is perfect if you want to taste what real pizza should be like. My wife always gets the Prosciutto and Arugula, and I'm partial to whatever seasonal special they're running. The antipasti plates are also worth getting if you're sharing with friends.

It gets busy on weekends, so either order ahead for pickup or be ready to wait a bit. The Blue Stone Road location has plenty of parking, which beats fighting for spots at some of the other Italian places around here.
address: 6405 Blue Stone Rd Ste 240, Atlanta, GA 30328, USA
hours: Monday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM, Tuesday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM, Wednesday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM, Thursday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM, Friday: 11:30 AM – 11:00 PM, Saturday: 11:30 AM – 11:00 PM, Sunday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM

blurb:
This is where we go for anniversaries and special occasions when we want to feel fancy without driving downtown. The setting right on the Chattahoochee is gorgeous, especially if you can snag a window table. It's definitely a splurge, but the kind of place that makes you feel like you're celebrating something important.

The seafood here is legit. I always go with whatever fish they're featuring that night, and my husband loves their shrimp and grits. The Sunday brunch is also excellent if you want to make a whole afternoon of it with bottomless mimosas and river views.

Make a reservation, especially for dinner on weekends. The valet parking is worth it since the lot can be tricky to navigate in the dark.
address: 6700 Powers Ferry Rd NW, Sandy Springs, GA 30339, USA
hours: Monday: 11:30 AM – 10:00 PM, Tuesday: 11:30 AM – 10:00 PM, Wednesday: 11:30 AM – 10:00 PM, Thursday: 11:30 AM – 10:00 PM, Friday: 11:30 AM – 11:00 PM, Saturday: 12:00 – 11:00 PM, Sunday: 11:00 AM – 4:00 PM, 5:00 – 9:00 PM

blurb:
This food hall is perfect when you're with a group and everyone wants something different. It's right on the square, so you can walk around Marietta afterward or catch an event if something's happening. The setup reminds me of those fancy food halls in bigger cities, but with a small-town feel.

There are about twenty different vendors, so you can get everything from tacos to Asian food to desserts all in one place. It's great for families because kids can get chicken tenders while adults try something more adventurous. The seating is communal, which makes it feel social and lively.

It gets busy on weekends, especially when there's something happening on the square. Parking can be tricky during events, but it's worth walking a block or two. Perfect for a casual lunch or dinner when you want options.
address: 68 North Marietta Pkwy NW, Marietta, GA 30060, USA
hours: Mon-Thu 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM, Fri-Sat 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Sun 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM

blurb:
This place is a hidden gem if you're looking for authentic Middle Eastern food that won't break the bank. It's in a strip mall but don't let that fool you. The shawarma and falafel are exactly what you want when you're craving something different from the usual chain restaurants.

The shawarma wraps are massive and packed with flavor, and their falafel is crispy on the outside and perfectly seasoned. They have good vegetarian options too, which is great when you're trying to accommodate everyone in the group. The staff is friendly and will walk you through the menu if you're not familiar with Middle Eastern food.

It's perfect for a casual lunch or when you want to grab something quick for dinner. The prices are really reasonable for the portion sizes you get.
address: 1175 Franklin Gateway SE, Marietta, GA 30067, USA

blurb:
This French bakery is the real deal. The owner is actually French, and you can tell because everything tastes like it should. It's become my go-to for special occasion breakfasts or when I want to treat myself to proper pastries. The atmosphere is bright and welcoming, not stuffy like some French places can be.

Their croissants are buttery and flaky, exactly what you want in a croissant. The quiches are perfect for a light lunch, and the petits fours are beautiful if you need something for a party. Their baguettes are legit too, crusty on the outside and soft inside.

Get there early on weekends because the good stuff sells out. It's pricier than a chain bakery but worth it for the quality. Perfect for a special brunch or when you want to impress guests with authentic French pastries.
address: 277 South Marietta Pkwy SW, Marietta, GA 30064, USA
hours: Mon-Sat 7:00 AM – 6:00 PM, Sun 9:00 AM – 3:30 PM

blurb:
If you've never been to a Brazilian steakhouse, this is a great place to try it without the downtown Atlanta prices. It's all-you-can-eat, so come hungry. The servers walk around with different cuts of meat on skewers and slice it right at your table, which is fun to watch.

The cheese bread alone is worth the trip. It's warm, gooey, and addictive. The feijoada stew is authentic and hearty if you want to try something traditional. They also have a salad bar and sides, so even if you're not a huge meat eater, there's plenty to choose from.

This is perfect for celebrating something special or when you want to try a different dining experience. Just pace yourself because it's easy to fill up on the cheese bread before the good stuff comes around.
address: 1275 Powers Ferry Rd ste 230, Marietta, GA 30067, USA
hours: Mon-Thu 10:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Fri-Sat 10:00 AM – 11:00 PM, Sun 10:00 AM – 10:00 PM

blurb:
This is the kind of place that makes you feel like you're in a movie about diners. It's open 24/7, which is perfect when you have late-night cravings or need breakfast at 3am after a long shift. The neon sign and classic diner look pulls you right in, and the menu is absolutely massive.

Everyone talks about their breakfast platters, which are huge and reasonably priced. The Greek specialties are surprisingly good too since the owners know what they're doing. My family goes here for Sunday brunch when we want something dependable and filling.

Just know that it can get busy during typical meal times, but the turnover is pretty quick. It's cash and card friendly, and there's plenty of parking.
address: 306 Cobb Pkwy SE, Marietta, GA 30060, USA

blurb:
This is the taco place that finally made me stop settling for mediocre Mexican food. The atmosphere is lively and fun, perfect for date nights or hanging out with friends over drinks. They're open late most nights, which is clutch when you want real tacos after 9pm.

Start with the Esquite because it's addictive and shareable. The Pineapple Guava pitcher is dangerous in the best way, and the Passion Fruit pitcher is just as good. Both go down way too easy, so pace yourself if you're driving home.

The service here is consistently great, and they know their menu well enough to make good recommendations. It's in Sandy Springs Place, so parking is easy and you can walk around a bit after dinner if you need to work off those tacos.
address: 227 Sandy Springs Pl NE #506, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA
hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Thursday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Friday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Saturday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Sunday: 11:00 AM – 11:00 PM

blurb:
This place has officially made my top 5 dinner spots in Atlanta, and I don't say that lightly. It's perfect for girls' night out or date night when you want something different but still approachable. The vibe is upscale but not stuffy, and the service makes you feel like they actually want you there.

Start with the hummus and definitely try the wings, which sound weird for Persian food but trust me on this. The chicken shish kabobs with the rice that has lentils and raisins is what I dream about between visits. The cocktails are all solid, and save room for the tiramisu because it's the perfect end to the meal.

They break between lunch and dinner, so check the hours before you go. It's in the Aria complex on Blue Stone, so parking is never an issue and there's other stuff to do if you want to make a whole evening of it.
address: 6500 Aria Blvd Suite 500, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA
